coaching.coaching: Pin down ticket-comment acceptance criteria concrete form with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: ticket-comment acceptance criteria concrete form

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] actual unused-ignore locations in rebar package will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: actual unused-ignore locations in rebar package

coaching.coaching: Test the riskiest assumption behind dependency-freezing prerequisite completion status first.
coaching.subject: dependency-freezing prerequisite completion status

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Prefix both ticket-comment ACs with `[operator-attested]` per ADR 0043, or replace each with a repo-side artifact (e.g., a committed `docs/type-ignore-audit.md`) that CI or a reviewer can verify in-session.
evidence: No in-session command (grep, pytest, mypy) can verify that a ticket comment exists or contains the required content
The criteria are not evaluable via repo artifacts or closing PR CI
ADR 0043 defines the `[operator-attested]` tag precisely for this class of criterion
findings.finding: Two acceptance criteria require writing ticket comments ('Before remediation… recorded in a ticket comment' and 'A ticket comment maps each of the 23 baseline unused-ignore diagnostics…') but are not tagged `[operator-attested]` per ADR 0043, leaving them without any in-session proving command and non-evaluable via repo artifacts or CI.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Before remediation, the exact Mypy 2.3.0 redundant-cast command and its single editor.py:515 diagnostic are recorded in a ticket comment.' AND 'A ticket comment maps each of the 23 baseline unused-ignore diagnostics to removed, preserved-and-narrowed, or preserved-and-documented.'
scenarios: Reviewer cannot verify these ACs are satisfied from the PR alone; completion can only be confirmed by manually inspecting the ticket.
An automated CI gate checking AC completion would be unable to evaluate these criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Prefix: '[operator-attested] Before remediation, the exact Mypy 2.3.0 redundant-cast command and its single editor.py:515 diagnostic are recorded in a ticket comment.' and '[operator-attested] A ticket comment maps each of the 23 baseline unused-ignore diagnostics to removed, preserved-and-narrowed, or preserved-and-documented.' Alternatively, commit the audit log as docs/type-ignore-audit.md and assert its presence in the ratchet test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope file path from `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102` to match the actual location of the two `type: ignore[union-attr]` diagnostics.
evidence: src/rebar/__main__.py has 22 lines total and contains no `type: ignore` comment (grep confirmed zero matches)
src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70 and :102 both carry `spec.loader.exec_module(mod)  # type: ignore[union-attr]` (confirmed by read_file)
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section identifies `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` as one of the 23 unused-ignore diagnostic locations, but that file is only 22 lines long and contains zero `type: ignore` comments. The actual file with `type: ignore[union-attr]` at lines 70 and 102 is `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`. This incorrect path means the implementer will look in the wrong file for this pair of ignores.
findings.location: Scope section — 'The 23 observed warn_unused_ignores diagnostics' bullet: `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102`
scenarios: Implementer opens src/rebar/__main__.py, finds no ignores, cannot reconcile baseline, stalls work or incorrectly declares it already clean.

The canonical-environment unused-ignore run flags src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py, which the plan's AC-6 mapping step will not recognise as one of the 23 baseline diagnostics, leaving those two ignores unaccounted for.
findings.suggested_fix:
In the Scope section, replace:
```
- src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102
```
with:
```
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope path `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102` — the top-level `src/rebar/__main__.py` is 22 lines with no `type: ignore` comments.
evidence: `src/rebar/__main__.py` is 22 lines total (verified by read_file), with no `type: ignore` annotations anywhere.
`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` lines 70 and 102 both contain `spec.loader.exec_module(mod)  # type: ignore[union-attr]`, exactly matching the expected diagnostic pattern.
The Scope section item reads `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` — a path that cannot have lines 70 or 102.
findings.finding: The Scope section names `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` as one of the 23 unused-ignore diagnostic locations, but that file is only 22 lines long and contains zero `type: ignore` comments. The actual file with `type: ignore[union-attr]` at lines 70 and 102 is `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`.
findings.location: Scope section — 'The 23 observed warn_unused_ignores diagnostics' list: `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102`
scenarios: An implementer opens `src/rebar/__main__.py` looking for the ignore to remove or inspect, finds nothing (file ends at line 22), and either skips the diagnostic or applies changes to the wrong file.
The AC requiring a ticket comment mapping all 23 diagnostics could be satisfied against the wrong file path, leaving the actual ignore in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` unreviewed.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` with `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102` in the Scope section's 23-diagnostic list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope entry `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` to `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102` to match the actual file containing those `# type: ignore[union-attr]` annotations.
evidence: src/rebar/__main__.py contains only 22 lines and zero `type: ignore` annotations (confirmed by file read)
src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70 and :102 both contain `spec.loader.exec_module(mod)  # type: ignore[union-attr]` (confirmed by file read at lines 65-103)
The other 21 sites in the Scope list are all under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, making the `src/rebar/__main__.py` entry the outlier
findings.finding: The Scope section lists `src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` as one of the 23 unused-ignore sites, but `src/rebar/__main__.py` is a 22-line file with no `type: ignore` comments at any line; the actual sites at lines 70 and 102 exist in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`. An implementer following Step 5 ('Inspect the suppressed error … for each of the 23 unused-ignore diagnostics') will search the wrong file and find nothing, likely stalling remediation or silently skipping those two sites.
scenarios: Implementer opens src/rebar/__main__.py to inspect lines 70 and 102 for Step 5 triage, finds neither line nor any type: ignore, and either skips those sites or spends time debugging why Mypy reports them
If the two sites in rebar_reconciler/__main__.py are missed, `mypy src/rebar --warn-unused-ignores` continues to exit non-zero after all other remediations, blocking AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `- src/rebar/__main__.py:70,102` with `- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:70,102` in the Scope section.

evidence: 3 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a ticket ID or PR reference for the 'approved dependency-freezing work' prerequisite in Step 1 so an executor can confirm it is complete before proceeding with unused-ignore cleanup.
evidence: 'Depend on the approved dependency-freezing work' — no ticket number, branch name, or status signal given
Step 5 explicitly relies on 'canonical fully provisioned environment' which requires the freeze work to be complete
An executor proceeding with Step 5 without the freeze work risks cleaning ignores that are only stale in an under-provisioned environment
findings.finding: The plan depends on 'approved dependency-freezing work' (Step 1) but provides no ticket ID, PR reference, or completion status check. An executor cannot determine whether this prerequisite is satisfied before proceeding with Step 5's unused-ignore cleanup.
findings.location: Approach, Step 1: 'Depend on the approved dependency-freezing work to pin the canonical Mypy version and provision all project extras used by CI.'
scenarios: Executor proceeds with ignore cleanup in an incompletely provisioned environment, incorrectly removes ignores that are stale only there, introduces latent type errors in CI.
Executor blocks the entire story waiting for a dependency that is already merged.
findings.suggested_fix: State the default explicitly: 'This story depends on [TICKET-XYZ] (dependency-freeze work); confirm that ticket is merged and CI is pinned to Mypy 2.3.0 before beginning Step 5.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
